global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic outdoor scene of an old, weathered residential outdoor air conditioning condenser unit on the side of a Phoenix-style stucco home. The aging AC unit dominates the lower-right portion of the frame. The unit shows years of Arizona sun: faded paint, light surface rust at corners, dust caked into protective grille fins. Above and around the unit, intense Phoenix afternoon heat radiates visibly from the metal cabinet — heat shimmer rising off the top of the unit. Behind the unit, a tan stucco wall and beyond that a slice of pale sun-bleached sky with intense direct sunlight. Long harsh shadows stretch across a concrete equipment pad. Sunlight floods from the upper-right of the frame, creating dramatic glare and lens flare. The image has THREE text elements composited as part of the design: (1) a small narrow red horizontal sash spanning the top edge of the frame containing the bold all-caps white text 'PHOENIX HOMEOWNERS' centered, (2) very large bold all-caps white sans-serif headline text 'WILL THIS MAKE IT TO JULY?' positioned in the upper-third of the frame over the sky area, with a subtle dark drop-shadow behind it for legibility against the bright sky, (3) a tall solid bright red horizontal banner spanning the entire bottom edge of the frame, approximately 12% of the image height, containing the bold all-caps white text 'FREE IN-HOME ESTIMATE • 602-905-1450' centered in the banner. Photorealistic, shot on a 50mm lens, with the text overlays rendered crisply and cleanly as part of the final designed image.
global_context.weather_atmosphere: intense Phoenix heat, dry, dusty, heat shimmer in air

composition.focal_point: the headline 'WILL THIS MAKE IT TO JULY?' over the aged AC unit
